title: gypsocallis fragrans
intro: Gypsocallis fragrans, also known as the Fraser's magnolia, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its large, showy flowers and glossy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Gypsocallis fragrans include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy. Gypsocallis fragrans is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, although it can also be used in salves and ointments, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
